perturbation.original_text: Each party's obligations under this Agreement with respect to any portion of the other party's Confidential Information shall terminate when the Recipient can document that: (a) it was in the public domain at the time it was <PAGE> 2 communicated to the Recipient by the other party; (b) it entered the public domain subsequent to the time it was communicated to the Recipient by the other party through no fault of the Recipient; (c) it was in the Recipient's possession free of any obligation of confidence at the time it was communicated to the Recipient by the other party; (d) it was rightfully communicated to the Recipient free of any obligation of confidence subsequent to the time it was communicated to the Recipient by the other party or (e) it was communicated by the other party to an unaffiliated third party free of any obligation of confidence.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party's obligations under this Agreement with respect to any portion of the other party's Confidential Information shall terminate when the Recipient believes that: (a) it was in the public domain at the time it was <PAGE> 2 communicated to the Recipient by the other party; (b) it entered the public domain subsequent to the time it was communicated to the Recipient by the other party through no fault of the Recipient; (c) it was in the Recipient's possession free of any obligation of confidence at the time it was communicated to the Recipient by the other party; (d) it was rightfully communicated to the Recipient free of any obligation of confidence subsequent to the time it was communicated to the Recipient by the other party or (e) it was communicated by the other party to an unaffiliated third party free of any obligation of confidence.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by removing the requirement for the Recipient to 'document' the conditions for termination of confidentiality obligations, replacing it with a subjective belief standard ('Recipient believes that'). This creates uncertainty and potential disputes about when the confidentiality obligations actually terminate. This contradicts the original intention of providing a clear, objective standard for terminating confidentiality, creating an in-text contradiction about what exactly is required for the confidential information to not be confidential anymore.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that at all times until termination or expiration of this Agreement it will hold in strict confidence and not disclose to any third party Confidential Information of the other, except as approved in writing by the other party to this Agreement, and will use the Confidential Information for no purpose other than evaluating or pursuing a business relationship with the other party to this Agreement. Notwithstanding the above, the party to whom Confidential Information was disclosed (the "RECIPIENT") shall not be in violation of this Section 3 with regard to a disclosure that was in response to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, provided that the Recipient provides the other party with prior written notice of such disclosure in order to permit the other party to seek confidential treatment of such information. Each party shall only permit access to Confidential Information of the other party to those of its employees or authorized representatives having a need to know and who have signed confidentiality agreements or are otherwise bound by confidentiality obligations at least as restrictive as those contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the objective standard of 'document' with the subjective standard of 'believes that.' This directly contradicts the original intent, as now the termination of confidentiality obligations relies on the recipient's belief, rather than concrete evidence. The contradicted text states that the party must 'hold in strict confidence,' which implies an objective standard. The changed text allows for a subjective assessment that may conflict with holding information in 'strict confidence' until certain documented conditions are met, creating a clear contradiction as the 'strict confidence' is now governed by the Recipient's subjective belief rather than documented proof. This falls under the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, specifically when different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination or expiration of the Agreement, or upon written request of the other party, each party shall promptly destroy or return to the other all documents and other tangible materials representing the other's Confidential Information and all copies thereof. The Recipient agrees to destroy all documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by the Recipient or its employees or representatives based on the information contained in the Confidential Information (except for references or summaries appearing in minutes or corporate records).
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination or expiration of the Agreement, each party may elect to either promptly destroy or return to the other all documents and other tangible materials representing the other's Confidential Information and all copies thereof, OR retain the confidential information indefinitely for archival purposes. The Recipient agrees to destroy all documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by the Recipient or its employees or representatives based on the information contained in the Confidential Information (except for references or summaries appearing in minutes or corporate records).
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by giving the party the option to either destroy or retain the confidential information even after termination or expiration. This directly contradicts the previous requirement that it should be 'promptly destroyed or returned', as now it can also be kept. This creates uncertainty about the obligations regarding the confidential information after termination or expiration of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon termination or expiration of the Agreement, each party may elect to either promptly destroy or return to the other all documents and other tangible materials representing the other's Confidential Information and all copies thereof, OR retain the confidential information indefinitely for archival purposes. The Recipient agrees to destroy all documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by the Recipient or its employees or representatives based on the information contained in the Confidential Information (except for references or summaries appearing in minutes or corporate records).
perturbation.justification: The original text requires prompt destruction or return of confidential information upon termination. The changed text introduces the option to 'retain the confidential information indefinitely for archival purposes', directly contradicting the original requirement. This creates a clear inconsistency and uncertainty about the obligation to destroy or return the information. The contradicted text in the contract repeats the same contradictory clause, so it is confirming that the contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Each party acknowledges that its breach of the Agreement will cause irreparable damage and hereby agrees that the other party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief under this Agreement, as well as such further relief as may be granted by a court of competent jurisdiction. Additionally, in the event of a breach by the Recipient, the disclosing party shall be entitled to recover the costs of enforcing this Agreement including, without limitation reasonable attorneys' fees.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party acknowledges that its breach of the Agreement may or may not cause irreparable damage. The parties agree to resolve any disputes through binding arbitration. The disclosing party shall not be entitled to recover the costs of enforcing this Agreement, including reasonable attorneys' fees.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding remedies for breach of contract. It states that the breach may or may not cause irreparable damage, eliminating the automatic right to seek injunctive relief, and replaces court action with binding arbitration. Furthermore, it removes the right to recover enforcement costs, including attorney's fees. This creates uncertainty about the enforcement mechanism and contradicts the original understanding of potential remedies for breach by stating opposite terms, causing an in-text contradiction within the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party acknowledges that its breach of the Agreement may or may not cause irreparable damage. The parties agree to resolve any disputes through binding arbitration. The disclosing party shall not be entitled to recover the costs of enforcing this Agreement, including reasonable attorneys' fees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the remedies available in case of a breach. The original text guarantees injunctive relief and recovery of enforcement costs, including attorney's fees. The changed text states that irreparable damage may or may not occur, replaces court action with binding arbitration, and removes the right to recover enforcement costs. The contradicted text is actually a copy of the changed text, representing that this change strongly contradicts the original meaning of the agreement, creating uncertainty about enforcement and conflicting with the initial understanding of potential remedies, thereby fulfilling the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: All notices or reports permitted or required under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be delivered by personal delivery, electronic mail, facsimile transmission or by certified or registered mail, return receipt requested, and shall be deemed given upon personal delivery, five (5) days after deposit in the mail, or upon acknowledgment of receipt of electronic transmission. Notices shall be sent to the addresses set forth at the end of this Agreement or such other address as either party may specify in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: All notices or reports permitted or required under this Agreement shall be delivered by personal delivery ONLY. Notices shall be sent to the addresses set forth at the end of this Agreement or such other address as either party may specify in writing. A follow up call, is only deemed to be an acceptable notice, if the opposite part confirms the contents of that call via writing or electronic email.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by restricting the method of delivering notices. It limits all communications to personal delivery and introducing new form of follow up call, while the initial paragraph had the option of electronic mail, facsimile transmission or certified or registered mail. This restriction creates uncertainty about whether the other methods stated in the initial paragraph are considered applicable, causing an in-text contradiction within the text regarding the permitted channels for communication, creating uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All notices or reports permitted or required under this Agreement shall be delivered by personal delivery ONLY. Notices shall be sent to the addresses set forth at the end of this Agreement or such other address as either party may specify in writing. A follow up call, is only deemed to be an acceptable notice, if the opposite part confirms the contents of that call via writing or electronic email.
perturbation.justification: The changed text restricts the acceptable forms of communication to personal delivery and a follow-up call confirmed in writing, while the original text allowed for electronic mail, facsimile transmission, or certified mail. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, reinforcing this restriction. This creates a direct contradiction within the agreement regarding acceptable methods of delivering notices, thus resulting in an inconsistency as defined in the instructions.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be executed in two or more counterparts, each of which when so executed and delivered shall be deemed an original, and such counterparts together shall constitute only one instrument.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement must be executed in a single original document and cannot be executed in counterparts.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by directly contradicting the clause that allows the agreement to be executed in counterparts. Now, the agreement must be in a single original, while the original text allows for counterparts. This introduces immediate uncertainty about the validity of the agreement if counterparts are used, creating an in-text contradiction on the manner by which the agreement can be executed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement must be executed in a single original document and cannot be executed in counterparts.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the agreement must be executed in a single original document and cannot be executed in counterparts. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, emphasizing the contradiction. The original text allowed for counterparts, but the change makes that impossible, creating a direct conflict within the agreement regarding execution methods.
